title: Lemma A(i,ii)+G4: real-rootedness & commutation identity for boxplus_n and T_eps

comments.author: human
comments.body:
Structural prerequisites for the reduction and the whole argument:

**(a) Real-rootedness preservation.** $p\boxplus_n q$ is real-rooted whenever $p,q$ are (Marcus-Spielman-Srivastava interlacing families; Marcus, 'Polynomial convolutions and finite free probability'). State precisely and cite/derive; it is needed for $\Phi_n(p\boxplus_n q)$ to be a finite real quantity and for the score vectors to be real.

**(b) Commutation identity.** $(T_\epsilon p)\boxplus_n (T_\epsilon q) = T_\epsilon^2\,(p\boxplus_n q)$, where $T_\epsilon=(1-\epsilon\,d/dx)^n$.

**Verified numerically** (n=3, eps=0.37, random roots: exact coefficient match). Proof should follow from the differential-operator/umbral description of $\boxplus_n$ but must be written out explicitly (the operator $T_\epsilon$ acts via $(1-\epsilon D)$ which interacts with the bilinear coefficient formula $c_k=\sum_{i+j=k}\frac{(n-i)!(n-j)!}{n!(n-k)!}a_ib_j$).

**Why it matters:** this identity is what lets one prove $(\star)$ for $(T_\epsilon p,T_\epsilon q)$ (simple roots) and then transfer to $(p,q)$ via $\epsilon\to0$.
